name: Actor
sheetText:
You have advantage on Deception and Performance checks when trying to pass yourself off as a different person.
			You can mimic the speech of another person or the sounds made by other creatures. You must have heard the person speaking, or heard the creature make the sound, for at least 1 minute. A successful Insight check contested by your Deception check allows a listener to determine that the effect is faked.

name: Alert
sheetText:
You can’t be surprised while you are conscious. 
			Other creatures don’t gain advantage on attack rolls against you as a result of being unseen by you.

name: Athlete
sheetText:
When you are prone, standing up uses only 5 feet of your movement.
			Climbing doesn’t cost you extra movement.
			You can make a running long jump or a running high jump after moving only 5 feet on foot, rather than 10 feet.

name: Charger
sheetText:
When you use your action to Dash, you can use a bonus action to make one melee weapon attack or to shove a creature.
			If you move at least 10 feet in a straight line immediately before taking this bonus action, you either gain a +5 bonus to the attack’s damage roll (if you chose to make a melee attack and hit) or push the target up to 10 feet away from you (if you chose to shove and you succeed).

name: Crossbow Expert
sheetText:
You ignore the loading quality of crossbows with which you are proficient.
			Being within 5 feet of a hostile creature doesn’t impose disadvantage on your ranged attack rolls.
			When you use the Attack action and attack with a one-handed weapon, you can use a bonus action to attack with a hand crossbow you are holding.

name: Defensive Duelist
sheetText: When you are wielding a finesse weapon with which you are proficient and another creature hits you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to add {{defensive duelist:ac}} to your AC for that attack, potentially causing the attack to miss you.

name: Dual Wielder
sheetText:
+1 bonus to AC while you are wielding a separate melee weapon in each hand.
			You can use two-weapon fighting even when the one-handed melee weapons you are wielding aren’t light.
			You can draw or stow two one-handed weapons when you would normally be able to draw or stow only one.

name: Dungeon Delver
sheetText:
You have advantage on Perception and Investigation checks made to detect the presence of secret doors.
			You have advantage on saving throws made to avoid or resist traps.
			You have resistance to the damage dealt by traps.
			Traveling at a fast pace doesn’t impose the normal −5 penalty on your passive Perception score.

name: Durable
sheetText: When you roll a Hit Die to regain hit points, the minimum number of hit points you regain from the roll equals {{durable:hd:bonus}}.

name: Grappler
sheetText:
You have advantage on attack rolls against a creature you are grappling.
			You can use your action to try to pin a creature grappled by you. To do so, make another grapple check. If you succeed, you and the creature are both restrained until the grapple ends.

name: Great Weapon Master
sheetText:
On your turn, when you score a critical hit with a melee weapon or reduce a creature to 0 hit points with one, you can make one melee weapon attack as a bonus action.
			Before you make a melee attack with a heavy weapon that you are proficient with, you can choose to take a - 5 penalty to the attack roll. If the attack hits, you add +10 to the attack’s damage.

name: Healer
sheetText:
You can spend one use of a healer’s kit to tend to a creature and restore 1d6 + 4 hit points to it, plus additional hit points equal to the creature’s maximum number of Hit Dice. The creature can’t regain hit points from this feat again until it finishes a short or long rest.
			When you use a healer’s kit to stabilize a dying creature, that creature also regains 1 hit point.

name: Heavy Armor Master
sheetText: While you are wearing heavy armor, bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage that you take from nonmagical attacks is reduced by 3.

name: Inspiring Leader
sheetText: You can spend 10 minutes inspiring your companions, shoring up their resolve to fight. When you do so, choose up to six friendly creatures within 30 feet of you who can see or hear you and who can understand you. Each creature can gain {{inspiring leader:hp:temp}} temporary hit points. A creature can’t gain temporary hit points from this feat again until it has finished a short or long rest.

name: Keen Mind
sheetText:
You always know which way is north.
			You always know the number of hours left before the next sunrise or sunset.
			You can accurately recall anything you have seen or heard within the past month.

name: Lightly Armored
sheetText: You have trained to master the use of light armor.

name: Linguist
sheetText: You can ably create written ciphers. Others can’t decipher a code you create unless you teach them, they succeed on an Intelligence check (DC {{linguist:dc}}), or they use magic to decipher it.

name: Lucky
sheetText:
Whenever you make an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw, you can spend one luck point to roll an additional d20. You can choose to spend one of your luck points after you roll the die, but before the outcome is determined.
			You can also spend one luck point when an attack roll is made against you. Roll a d20, and then choose whether the attack uses the attacker’s roll or yours.

name: Mage Slayer
sheetText:
When a creature within 5 feet of you casts a spell, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature.
			When you damage a creature that is concentrating on a spell, that creature has disadvantage on the saving throw it makes to maintain its concentration.
			You have advantage on saving throws against spells cast by creatures within 5 feet of you.

name: Martial Adept
sheetText: {{martial adept:dice:amount}}d{{martial adept:dice:size}} superiority dice / short rest. DC {{martial adept:dc}}

name: Medium Armor Master
sheetText: Wearing medium armor doesn’t impose disadvantage on your Stealth checks.

name: Mobile
sheetText:
When you use the Dash action, difficult terrain doesn’t cost you extra movement on that turn.
			When you make a melee attack against a creature, you don’t provoke opportunity attacks from that creature for the rest of the turn, whether you hit or not.

name: Mounted Combatant
sheetText:
You have advantage on melee attack rolls against any unmounted creature that is smaller than your mount.
			You can force an attack targeted at your mount to target you instead. 
			If your mount is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw, and only half damage if it fails.

name: Observant
sheetText:
If you can see a creature’s mouth while it is speaking a language you understand, you can interpret what it’s saying by reading its lips.
			You have a +5 bonus to your passive Perception and passive Investigation scores.

name: Polearm Master
sheetText:
When you take the Attack action and attack with only a glaive, halberd, quarterstaff, or spear, you can use a bonus action to make a melee attack with the opposite end of the weapon. The weapon’s damage die for this attack is a d4, and the attack deals bludgeoning damage. This attack uses the same ability modifier as the primary attack.
			While you are wielding a glaive, halberd, pike, quarterstaff, or spear, other creatures provoke an opportunity attack from you when they enter your reach.

name: Ritual Caster
sheetText: You have learned a number of spells that you can cast as rituals.

name: Savage Attacker
sheetText: Once per turn when you roll damage for a melee weapon attack, you can reroll the weapon's damage dice as use either total.

name: Sentinel
sheetText:
When you hit a creature with an opportunity attack, the creature’s speed becomes 0 for the rest of the turn.
			Creatures provoke opportunity attacks from you even if they take the Disengage action before leaving your reach.
			When a creature within 5 feet of you makes an attack against a target other than you, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against the attacking creature.

name: Sharpshooter
sheetText:
Attacking at long range doesn’t impose disadvantage on your ranged weapon attack rolls.
			Your ranged weapon attacks ignore half cover and three-quarters cover.
			Before you make an attack with a ranged weapon that you are proficient with, you can choose to take a - 5 penalty to the attack roll. If the attack hits, you add +10 to the attack’s damage.

name: Shield Master
sheetText:
If you take the Attack action on your turn, you can use a bonus action to try to shove a creature within 5 feet of you with your shield.
			If you aren’t incapacitated, you can add your shield’s AC bonus to any Dexterity saving throw you make against a spell or other harmful effect that targets only you.
			If you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you can use your reaction to take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, interposing your shield between yourself and the source of the effect.

name: Skulker
sheetText:
You can try to hide when you are lightly obscured from the creature from which you are hiding.
			When you are hidden from a creature and miss it with a ranged weapon attack, making the attack doesn’t reveal your position.
			Dim light doesn’t impose disadvantage on your Perception checks relying on sight.

name: Spell Sniper
sheetText:
When you cast a spell that requires you to make an attack roll, the spell’s range is doubled.
			Your ranged spell attacks ignore half cover and three-quarters cover.

name: Tavern Brawler
sheetText:
You are proficient with improvised weapons.
			Your unarmed strike uses a d4 for damage.
			When you hit a creature with an unarmed strike or an improvised weapon on your turn, you can use a bonus action to attempt to grapple the target.

name: Tough
sheetText: Your hit points maximum increases by {{tough:hp}}.

name: War Caster
sheetText:
You have advantage on Constitution saving throws that you make to maintain your concentration on a spell when you take damage.
			You can perform the somatic components of spells even when you have weapons or a shield in one or both hands.
			When a hostile creature’s movement provokes an opportunity attack from you, you can use your reaction to cast a spell at the creature, rather than making an opportunity attack. The spell must have a casting time of 1 action and must target only that creature.

name: Blessed Warrior
sheetText: Whenever you gain a Paladin level, you can replace one of the chosen cantrips with another Cleric cantrip.

name: Druidic Warrior
sheetText: Whenever you gain a Ranger level, you can replace one of the chosen cantrips with another Druid cantrip.

name: Boon of Combat Prowess
sheetText: When you miss with an attack roll, you can hit instead.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Boon of Combat Prowess)

name: Boon of Dimensional Travel
sheetText: Immediately after you take the Attack action or the Magic action, you can teleport up to 30ft. to an unoccupied space you can see.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Boon of Dimensional Travel)

name: Boon of Energy Resistance
sheetText: When you take damage of one of the types chosen for this Boon, you can direct damage of the same type toward another creature you can see within 60ft. of yourself that isn't behind Total Cover. If you do so, that creature must succeed on a DC{{energy resistance:dc}} Dexterity saving throw or take 2d12+{{constitution:modifier}} damage.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Boon of Energy Resistance)

name: Boon of Fate
sheetText: When you or another creature within 60ft. of you succeeds on or fails a D20 Test, you can roll 2d4 and apply the total rolled as a bonus or penalty to the d20 roll. You also regain a use of this feature whenever you roll Initiative.

name: Boon of Fortitude
sheetText: Whenever you regain HPs, you can regain {{constitution:modifier}} additional HPs.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Boon of Irresistible Offense)

name: Boon of Recovery
sheetText:
When you would be reduced to 0 HP, you can drop to 1 HP instead and regain a number of HPs equal to half your HP maximum. 
			Additionally, you have a pool of 10d10s. As a Bonus Action, you can expend dice from the pool, roll those dice, and regain a number of HPs equal to the roll's total. You regain all the expended dice when you finish a Long Rest.

name: Boon of Speed
sheetText: You can take the Disengage action, which also ends the Grappled condition on you.

name: Boon of Spell Recall
sheetText: Whenever you cast a spell with a level 1-4 spell slot, roll 1d4. If the number you roll is the same as the slot's level, the slot isn't expended.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Boon of Spell Recall)

name: Boon of the Night Spirit
sheetText:
While within Dim Light or Darkness, you can become Invisible. The condition ends on you immediately after you take an action, a Bonus Action, or a Reaction. 
			Additionally, while within Dim Light or Darkness, you have Resistance to all damage except Psychic and Radiant.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Boon of the Night Spirit)

name: Dueling
sheetText: When you're holding a Melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

name: Great Weapon Fighting
sheetText: When you roll a 1 or 2 on a damage die for an attack you make with a melee weapon that you are wielding with two hands, you can treat the result as a 3. The weapon must have the Two-Handed or Versatile property to gain this benefit.

name: Interception
sheetText: When a creature you can see hits another creature within 5ft. of you with an attack roll, you can reduce the damage dealt to the target by 1d10+{{proficiency}}. You must be holding a Shield or a Simple or Martial weapon.

name: Protection
sheetText: When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within 5ft. of you, you can impose Disadvantage on the attack roll and all other attack rolls against the target until the start of your next turn if you remain within 5ft. of the target. You must be holding a Shield.

name: Thrown Weapon Fighting
sheetText: You gain a +2 bonus to the damage of a ranged attack using a weapon that has the Thrown property.

name: Two-Weapon Fighting
sheetText: You can add your ability modifier to the extra attack damage of a weapon that has the Light property.

name: Unarmed Fighting
sheetText:
When you hit with your Unarmed Strike and deal damage, you can deal 1d6 (or 1d8, if aren't holding any weapons or a Shield) + {{strength:modifier}} Bludgeoning damage instead of the normal damage of an Unarmed Strike. 
			At the start of each of your turns, you can deal 1d4 Bludgeoning damage to one creature Grappled by you.

name: Actor
sheetText:
While you're disguised as a real or fictional person, you have Advantage on Charisma (Deception or Performance) checks to convince others that you are that person. 
			Additionally, you can mimic the sounds of other creatures, including speech. A creature that hears the mimicry must succeed on a Wisdom (Insight) check against DC{{actor:dc}} to determine the effect is faked.

name: Athlete
sheetText: When you have the Prone condition, you can right yourself with only 5 feet of movement. You can make a running Long or High Jump after moving only 5 feet.

name: Charger
sheetText:
If you move at least 10ft. in a straight line toward a target immediately before hitting it with a melee attack roll as part of the Attack action, choose one of the following effects: gain a 1d8 bonus to the attack's damage roll, or push the target up to 10ft. away if it is no more than one size larger than you. 
			Additionally, when you take the Dash action, your Speed increases by 10 feet for that action.

name: Chef
sheetText:
As part of a Short Rest, if you have ingredients and Cook's Utensils on hand, you can prepare enough of food for {{chef:amount}} creatures. Creature who eats the food and spends one or more Hit Dice to regain HP regains an extra 1d8 HP. 
			Additionally, with 1 hour of work or when you finish a Long Rest, you can cook {{proficiency}} treats if you have ingredients and Cook's Utensils on hand. These special treats last 8 hours. A creature can use a Bonus Action to eat one of those treats to gain {{proficiency}} THP.

name: Crossbow Expert
sheetText: You ignore the Loading property of crossbows. If you're holding one of them, you can load a piece of ammunition into it even if you lack a free hand. Being within 5ft. of an enemy doesn't impose Disadvantage on your attack rolls with crossbows. When you make the extra attack with a crossbow that has the Light property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of the extra attack if you aren't already adding that modifier to the damage.

name: Crusher
sheetText:
When you hit a creature with an attack that deals Bludgeoning damage, you can move it 5ft. to an unoccupied space if the target is no more than one size larger than you. 
			Additionally, whenever you score a Critical Hit that deals Bludgeoning damage to a creature, attack rolls against that creature have Advantage until the start of your next turn.

name: Defensive Duelist
sheetText: If you're holding a Finesse weapon and another creature hits you with a melee attack, you can add {{proficiency}} to your AC, potentially causing the attack to miss you. This bonus to AC lasts until the start of your next turn.

name: Dual Wielder
sheetText:
When you attack with a weapon that has the Light property, you can make one extra attack as a Bonus Action with a different Melee weapon that lacks the Two-Handed property. You don't add your ability modifier to the extra attack's damage unless that modifier is negative. 
			Whenever you draw or stow your weapons that lack the Two-handed property, you can do so with two weapons instead of only one.

name: Durable
sheetText:
You can expend one of your Hit Dice, roll the die, and regain a number of HP equal to the roll. 
			Additionally, you have Advantage on Death Saving Throws.

name: Grappler
sheetText:
You gain following benefits: 
			• Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike as part of the Attack action on your turn, you can use both the Damage and the Grapple option. 
			• You have Advantage on attack rolls against a creature Grappled by you. 
			• You don’t have to spend extra movement to move a creature Grappled by you if the creature is your size or smaller.

name: Great Weapon Master
sheetText:
Whenever you deal damage to a creature with a Heavy weapon as part of the Attack action, you can deal an extra {{proficiency}} damage to it. 
			Additionally, after you score a Critical Hit with a Melee weapon or reduce a creature to 0 HP with one, you can make one attack with the same weapon as a Bonus Action.

name: Heavy Armor Master
sheetText: While wearing Heavy armor, any Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage you receive from an attack is reduced by {{proficiency}}.

name: Keen Mind
sheetText: You can take the Study action.

name: Mage Slayer
sheetText:
If you fail an Intelligence, a Wisdom, or a Charisma saving throw, you can cause yourself to succeed instead. 
			Additionally, when you damage a creature that is concentrating, it has Disadvantage on the saving throw it makes to maintain Concentration.

name: Mounted Combatant
sheetText:
While mounted, you have Advantage on attack rolls against any unmounted creature within 5 feet of your mount that is at least one size smaller than the mount. 
			When your mount makes a Dexterity saving throw to take half damage from an effect, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds and only half damage if it fails. For your mount to gain this benefit, you must be riding it, and neither of you can have the Incapacitated condition. 
			While mounted, you can force an attack that hits your mount to hit you instead if you don't have the Incapacitated condition.

name: Observant
sheetText: You can take the Search action.

name: Piercer
sheetText:
When you hit a creature with an attack that deals Piercing damage, you can reroll one of the attack's damage dice, and you must use the new roll. 
			Additionally, whenever you score a Critical Hit that deals Piercing damage to a creature, you can roll one additional damage die when determining the extra Piercing damage.

name: Polearm Master
sheetText:
Immediately after you take the Attack action and attack with a Quarterstaff, a Spear, or a weapon that has the Heavy and Reach properties, you can use a Bonus Action to make a melee attack with the opposite end of the weapon. The weapon deals Bludgeoning damage, and the weapon's damage die for this attack is a d4. 
			Additionally, while you're holding a Quarterstaff, a Spear, or a weapon that has the Heavy and Reach properties, you can take a Reaction to make one melee attack against a creature that enters the reach you have with that weapon.

name: Sentinel
sheetText:
Immediately after a creature within 5ft. of you takes the Disengage action or hits a target other than you with an attack, you can make an Opportunity Attack against that creature. 
			Additionally, whenever you hit a creature with an Opportunity Attack, the creature's Speed becomes 0 for the rest of the current turn.

name: Sharpshooter
sheetText: Your gain the following benefits, when making ranged weapon attacks: Being within 5ft. of an enemy or attacking at long range doesn't impose Disadvantage; you can also ignore Half Cover and Three-Quarters Cover.

name: Shield Master
sheetText:
When you hit a creature within 5ft of you with a Melee weapon as part of the Attack action, you can immediately bash the target with your Shield if it's equipped, forcing the target to make a DC{{shield master:dc}} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, you either push the target 5ft. from you or cause it to have the Prone condition. 
			Additionally, if you succeed on a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage while holding a Shield, you can use a Reaction to take no damage instead.

name: Skulker
sheetText: You have Advantage on any Dexterity (Stealth) check you make as part of the Hide action during combat. Additionally while hidden, you don't reveal yourself when you miss an attack roll.

name: Slasher
sheetText:
When you hit a creature with an attack that deals Slashing damage, you can reduce the Speed of that creature by 10ft. until the start of your next turn. 
			Additionally, whenever you score a Critical Hit that deals Slashing damage to a creature, it has Disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of your next turn.

name: Speedy
sheetText: When you take the Dash action on your turn, Difficult Terrain doesn't cost you extra movement for the rest of that turn. Additionally, Opportunity Attacks have Disadvantage against you.

name: Spell Sniper
sheetText:
Your spell attacks gain following benefits: Being within 5ft. of an enemy doesn't impose Disadvantage; you can also ignore Half Cover and Three-Quarters Cover. 
			Additionally, when you cast a spell that has a range of at least 10ft. and requires you to make an attack roll, you can increase the spell's range by 60ft.

name: War Caster
sheetText:
You have Advantage on saving throws to maintain Concentration. 
			You can perform the Somatic components of spells even when you have weapons or a Shield in one or both hands. 
			When a creature provokes an Opportunity Attack from you by leaving your reach, cast a spell at the creature as a Reaction, rather than making an Opportunity Attack. The spell must have a casting time of 1 action and must target only that creature.

name: Alert
sheetText: Immediately after you roll Initiative, you can swap yours with that of one willing ally in the same combat. You can't make this swap if you or the ally has the Incapacitated condition.

name: Crafter
sheetText: Whenever you buy a nonmagical item, you receive a 20% discount on it. When you finish a Long Rest, you can craft one piece of gear from the Fast Crafting table, provided you have tools and proficiency associated with that item. The item lasts until you finish another Long Rest.

name: Healer
sheetText:
You gain the following benefits: 
			• You can spend one use of a Healer's Kit to tend to a creature within 5ft. of yourself. That creature can expend one of its Hit Dice, and you then roll that die. It regains a number of HP equal to the roll + {{proficiency}}. 
			• Whenever you roll a 1 on a die to restore HP with a spell or the benefit above, you can reroll it, you must use the new result.

name: Lucky
sheetText:
When you roll a d20 for a D20 Test, you can give yourself Advantage on the roll.
			Alternatively, when a creature rolls a d20 for an attack roll against you, you can impose Disadvantage on that roll.

name: Musician
sheetText: As you finish a Short or Long Rest, you can play a song on a Musical Instrument you are proficient with and give Heroic Inspiration to up to {{proficiency}} allies who hear the song.

name: Savage Attacker
sheetText: When you hit a target with a weapon, you can roll the weapon's damage dice twice and use either roll against the target.

name: Tavern Brawler
sheetText:
You gain following benefits: 
			• When you hit with your Unarmed Strike and deal damage, you can deal 1d4+{{strength:modifier}} Bludgeoning damage, and once per turn you can also push the target of that attack 5ft. away from you. 
			• Whenever you roll a 1 for damage for your Unarmed Strike, you can reroll the die, and you must use the new roll.

name: Svirfneblin Magic
sheetText: Cast nondetection on yourself at will. Cast each of the following spells once: blindness/deafness, blur, and disguise self. (1/LR) (Spellcasting: Intelligence)

name: Bountiful Luck
sheetText: When an ally you can see within 30 feet of you rolls a 1 on the d20 for an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw, you can use let the ally reroll the die. The ally must use the new roll. When you use this ability, you can’t use your Lucky racial trait before the end of your next turn.

name: Dragon Fear
sheetText: Instead of exhaling destructive energy, you can expend a use of your Breath Weapon trait to roar, forcing each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC{{dragon fear:dc}}). A target automatically succeeds on the save if it can’t hear or see you. On a failed save, a target becomes frightened of you for 1 minute. If the frightened target takes any damage, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.

name: Dragon Hide
sheetText:
While you aren’t wearing armor, your AC is {{dragon hide:natural armor}}. You can use a shield and still gain this benefit. 
			You grow retractable claws. Extending or retracting the claws requires no action. The claws are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes that deal 1d4+{{strength:modifier}} slashing damage.

name: Drow High Magic
sheetText: You learn the detect magic spell and can cast it at will, without expending a spell slot. You also learn levitate and dispel magic, each of which you can cast once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to cast those two spells in this way when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for all three spells.

name: Dwarven Fortitude
sheetText: You can spend one Hit Die to heal yourself. Roll the die, add {{constitution:modifier}}, and regain a number of hit points equal to the total (minimum of 1).

name: Elven Accuracy
sheetText: Whenever you have advantage on an attack roll using Dexterity, Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma, you can reroll one of the dice once.

name: Fade Away
sheetText: Immediately after you take damage, you can magically become invisible until the end of your next turn or until you attack, deal damage, or force someone to make a saving throw.

name: Fey Teleportation
sheetText: You can cast the misty step spell without expending a spell slot. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

name: Flames of Phlegethos
sheetText: When you roll fire damage for a spell you cast, you can reroll any roll of 1 on the fire damage dice, you must use the new roll. Whenever you cast a spell that deals fire damage, you can cause flames to wreathe you until the end of your next turn. The flames don’t harm you or your possessions, and they shed bright light out to 30 feet and dim light for an additional 30 feet. While the flames are present, any creature within 5 feet of you that hits you with a melee attack takes 1d4 fire damage.

name: Infernal Constitution
sheetText: You have advantage on saving throws against being poisoned.

name: Orcish Fury
sheetText: When you hit with an attack using a simple or martial weapon, you can roll one of the weapon’s damage dice an additional time and add it as extra damage of the weapon’s damage type. Additionally, immediately after you use your Relentless Endurance trait, you can use your reaction to make one weapon attack.

name: Second Chance
sheetText: When a creature you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can force that creature to reroll. Once you use this ability, you can’t use it again until you roll initiative at the start of combat or until you finish a short or long rest.

name: Squat Nimbleness
sheetText: You have advantage on any Athletics or Acrobatics check you make to escape from being grappled.

name: Wood Elf Magic
sheetText: You learn the long strider and pass without trace spells, each of which you can cast once without expending a spell slot. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for chosen cantrip and these spells.

name: Revenant Blade
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War
sheetText:
While you are holding a double-bladed scimitar with two hands, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.
			A double-bladed scimitar has the finesse property when you wield it.

name: Aberrant Dragonmark
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War
sheetText:
You learn spells and can cast them through your mark using Constitution as your spellcasting ability.
			When you cast the 1st-level spell through your mark, you can expend one of your Hit Dice and roll it. If you roll an even number, you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to the number rolled. If you roll an odd number, one random creature within 30 feet of you (not including you) takes force damage equal to the number rolled. If no other creatures are in range, you take the damage.

name: Artificer Initiate
sheetText: You can cast this feat’s 1st-level spell without a spell slot once. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have. This feat's cantrip and spell use Intelligence as your spellcasting ability. You can use this feat’s type of tool as a spellcasting focus for any spell you cast that uses Intelligence as its spellcasting ability.

name: Chef
sheetText:
As part of a short rest, you can cook special food provided you have ingredients and cook’s utensils on hand. You can prepare enough of this food for {{chef:creatures}} creatures. At the end of the short rest, any creature who eats the food and spends one or more Hit Dice to regain hp regains an extra 1d8 hp. 
			With one hour of work or when you finish a long rest, you can cook {{proficiency}} treats. These special treats last 8 hours after being made. A creature can use a bonus action to eat one of those treats to gain {{proficiency}} temporary hp.

name: Crusher
sheetText: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack that deals bludgeoning damage, you can move it 5 feet to an unoccupied space, provided the target is no more than one size larger than you. When you score a critical hit that deals bludgeoning damage to a creature, attack rolls against that creature are made with advantage until the start of your next turn.

name: Fighting Initiate
sheetText: Your martial training has helped you develop a particular style of fighting. When you gain Ability Score Improvement feature, you can replace this feat’s fighting style with another one.

name: Gunner
sheetText: You ignore the loading property of firearms. Being within 5 feet of a hostile creature doesn’t impose disadvantage on your ranged attack rolls.

name: Metamagic Adept
sheetText: You can use only one Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it, unless the option says otherwise. You gain 2 sorcery points to spend on Metamagic. You regain all spent sorcery points when you finish a long rest.

name: Piercer
sheetText: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack that deals piercing damage, you can reroll one of the attack’s damage dice, and you must use the new roll. When you score a critical hit that deals piercing damage to a creature, you can roll one additional damage die when determining the extra piercing damage the target takes.

name: Poisoner
sheetText:
When you make a damage roll that deals poison damage, it ignores resistance to poison damage. 
			You can apply poison to a weapon or piece of ammunition as a bonus action, instead of an action. 
			With one hour of work using a poisoner’s kit and expending 50 gp worth of materials, you can create {{proficiency}} doses of potent poison. Once applied to a weapon or piece of ammunition, the poison retains its potency for 1 minute or until you hit with the weapon or ammunition. When a creature takes damage from the coated weapon or ammunition, that creature must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or take 2d8 poison damage and become poisoned until the end of your next turn.

name: Slasher
sheetText:
Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack that deals slashing damage, you can reduce the speed of the target by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.
			When you score a critical hit that deals slashing damage to a creature, you grievously wound it. Until the start of your next turn, the target has disadvantage on all attack rolls.

name: Strixhaven Mascot
sheetText:
You have learned how to summon a Strixhaven mascot to assist you, granting you these benefits:
			• You can cast the find familiar spell as a ritual. Your familiar can take the form of the mascot associated with the college you chose for the Strixhaven Initiate feat.
			• When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can forgo one attack to allow your mascot familiar to make one attack of its own with its reaction.
			• If your mascot familiar is within 60 feet of you, you can teleport as an action, swapping places with the familiar. If your destination space is too small for you to occupy, the teleportation fails and is wasted. Once you teleport in this way, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot of 2nd level or higher to do it again.

name: Adept of the Black Robes
source: Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen

name: Adept of the Red Robes
source: Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen

name: Adept of the White Robes
source: Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen

name: Divinely Favored
source: Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen

name: Initiate of High Sorcery
source: Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen

name: Knight of the Crown
source: Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Knight of the Crown)

name: Knight of the Rose
source: Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Knight of the Rose)

name: Knight of the Sword
source: Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Knight of the Rose)

name: Squire of Solamnia
source: Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen

name: Ember of the Fire Giant
source: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants
sheetText: Once on your turn, each creature of your choice in a 15-foot-radius sphere centered on you must make a Dexterity saving throw DC{{searing ignition:dc}}. On a failed save, a creature takes fire damage equal to 1d8 + {{proficiency}}, and it has the blinded condition until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Ember of the Fire Giant)

name: Fury of the Frost Giant
source: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants
sheetText: Immediately after a creature you can see within 30 feet of you hits you with an attack roll and deals damage, you can retaliate with a conjured blast of ice. The creature must make a Constitution saving throw DC{{frigid retaliation:dc}}. On a failed save, the creature takes cold damage equal to 1d8 + {{proficiency}}, and its speed is reduced to 0 until the end of its next turn.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Fury of the Frost Giant)

name: Guile of the Cloud Giant
source: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants
sheetText: When a creature you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can give yourself resistance to that attack's damage. You then teleport to an unoccupied space that you can see within 30 feet of yourself.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Guile of the Cloud Giant)

name: Keenness of the Stone Giant
source: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Keenness of the Stone Giant)

name: Rune Shaper
source: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

name: Soul of the Storm Giant
source: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants
sheetText: You surround yourself with an aura of magical wind and lightning that extends 10 feet from you in every direction but not through total cover. The aura lasts until the start of your next turn or until you are incapacitated. While the aura is active, you have resistance to lightning and thunder damage. In addition, attack rolls against you have disadvantage, and whenever another creature starts its turn within the aura, you can force the creature to make a Strength saving throw DC{{maelstrom aura:dc}}. On a failed save, the creature's speed is halved until the start of its next turn.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Soul of the Storm Giant)

name: Strike of the Giants
source: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

name: Vigor of the Hill Giant
source: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants
sheetText:
You gain following benefits: 
			• Bulwark. When you are subjected to an effect that would move you at least 5 feet or give you the prone condition, you can use your reaction to steady yourself, ignoring the effect. 
			• Iron Stomach. Whenever you eat food as part of a short rest and spend Hit Dice to regain hit points, you regain additional {{iron stomach:hp}} hit points.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Guile of the Cloud Giant)

name: Agent of Order
sheetText: Once per turn, when you damage a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself, you can deal an extra 1d8 force damage to the target, and it must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC{{stasis strike:dc}}) as spectral bindings try to ensnare it. On a failed save, the target has the restrained condition until the start of your next turn. These bindings manifest as chains or some other symbol of stasis.

name: Baleful Scion
sheetText: Once per turn, when you damage a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself, you can also deal necrotic damage to it. The necrotic damage equals 1d6 + {{proficiency}}, and you regain a number of hit points equal to this necrotic damage dealt.

name: Cohort of Chaos
sheetText:
When you roll a 1 or a 20 on an attack roll or a saving throw, roll a d4: 
			On a 1, a creature of your choice that you can see is filled with reckless fury, it has advantage on attack rolls and disadvantage on ability checks; On a 2, every creature that starts its turn within 5 feet of you, or that moves into that area for the first time on a turn, takes 1d8 force damage; On a 3, when you move, you can use some or all of your walking speed to teleport yourself once, along with any equipment you're wearing or carrying, up to the distance used to an unoccupied space that you can see; On a 4, winds swirl in a 15-foot-radius sphere centered on you, you and any other creatures in that area have disadvantage on Wisdom saving throws. 
			A flare lasts until the end of your next turn, and a new flare can't occur until after the first flare ends.

name: Outlands Envoy
sheetText: You learn the misty step and tongues spells, you can cast each spell once using this feat without a spell slot, and you must finish a long rest before you can cast that spell in this way again. When you cast tongues using this feat, you require no material components. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spells' spellcasting ability is {{sotop:spellcasting}}.

name: Righteous Heritor
sheetText: When you or a creature within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can reduce that damage by 1d10 + {{proficiency}}.

choices.name: Plane Type (Scion of the Outer Planes)
Spellcasting Ability (Scion of the Outer Planes)

name: Cartomancer
sheetText: You can use prestidigitation to create illusions that duplicate the effects of stage magic. When you use prestidigitation in this way, you can conceal the verbal and somatic components of the spell as ordinary conversation and card handling.

name: Fell Handed
sheetText:
When using a handaxe, battleaxe, greataxe, warhammer or a maul, you gain +1 to attack rolls with that weapon. 
			Whenever you have advantage on a melee attack roll you make with the weapon and hit, you can knock the target prone if the lower of the two d20 rolls would also hit the target. Whenever you have disadvantage on a melee attack roll you make with the weapon, the target takes {{fell handed:damage}} bludgeoning damage if the attack misses but the higher of the two d20 rolls would have hit. 
			If you use the Help action to aid an ally’s melee attack while you’re wielding the weapon, you knock the target’s shield aside momentarily. In addition to the ally gaining advantage on the attack roll, the ally gains a +2 bonus to the roll if the target is using a shield.

name: Blade Mastery
sheetText:
When using a shortsword, longsword, scimitar, rapier, or a greatsword, you gain +1 to attack rolls with that weapon. 
			On your turn, you can use your reaction to assume a parrying stance, provided you have the weapon in hand. Doing so grants you a +1 bonus to your AC until the start of your next turn or until you’re not holding the weapon. When you make an opportunity attack with the weapon, you have advantage on the attack roll.

name: Flail Mastery
sheetText:
When using a flail, you gain +1 to attack rolls with that weapon.
			As a bonus action on your turn, you can prepare yourself to extend your flail to sweep over targets’ shields. Until the end of this turn, your attack rolls with a flail gain a +2 bonus against any target using a shield.
			When you hit with an opportunity attack using a flail, the target must succeed on a DC{{flail mastery:dc}} Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

name: Spear Mastery
sheetText:
When using a spear, you gain +1 to attack rolls with that weapon. 
			Its damage die changes from a d6 to a d8, and from a d8 to a d10 when wielded with two hands. (This benefit has no effect if another feature has already improved the weapon’s die.).
			You can set your spear to receive a charge. As a bonus action, choose a creature you can see that is at least 20 feet away from you. If that creatures moves within your spear’s reach on its next turn, you can make a melee attack against it with your spear as a reaction. If the attack hits, the target takes an extra 1d8 piercing damage, or an extra 1d10 piercing damage if you wield the spear with two hands.You can’t use this ability if the creature used the Disengage action before moving. 
			As a bonus action on your turn, you can increase your reach with a spear by 5 feet for the rest of your turn.

name: Alchemist
sheetText: As an action, you can identify one potion within 5 feet of you, as if you had tasted it. You must see the liquid for this benefit to work. Over the course of any short rest, you can temporarily improve the potency of one potion of healing of any rarity. To use this benefit, you must have alchemist’s supplies with you, and the potion must be within reach. If the potion is drunk no more than 1 hour after the short rest ends, the creature drinking the potion can forgo the potion’s die roll and regains the maximum number of hit points that the potion can restore.

name: Gourmand
sheetText: As an action, you can inspect a drink or plate of food within 5 feet of you and determine whether it is poisoned, provided that you can see and smell it. During a long rest, you can prepare and serve a meal that helps you and your allies recover from the rigors of adventuring, provided you have suitable food, cook’s utensils, and other supplies on hand. The meal serves up to six people, and each person who eats it regains two additional Hit Dice at the end of the long rest. In addition, those who partake of the meal have advantage on Constitution saving throws against disease for the next 24 hours.

name: Master of Disguise
sheetText: If you spend 1 hour observing a creature, you can then spend 8 hours crafting a disguise you can quickly don to mimic that creature. Making the disguise requires a disguise kit. You must make checks as normal to disguise yourself, but you can assume the disguise as an action.

name: Acrobat
sheetText: You can make a DC 15 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check. If you succeed, difficult terrain doesn’t cost you extra movement until the end of the current turn.

name: Animal Handler
sheetText: You can command one friendly beast within 60 feet of you that can hear you and that isn’t currently following the command of someone else. You decide now what action the beast will take and where it will move during its next turn, or you issue a general command that lasts for 1 minute, such as to guard a particular area.

name: Arcanist
sheetText: You can cast detect magic without expending a spell slot.

name: Diplomat
sheetText: If you spend 1 minute talking to someone who can understand what you say, you can make a Charisma (Persuasion) check contested by the creature’s Wisdom (Insight) check. If you or your companions are fighting the creature, your check automatically fails. If your check succeeds, the target is charmed by you as long as it remains within 60 feet of you and for 1 minute thereafter.

name: Empathic
sheetText: You can try to get uncanny insight about one humanoid you can see within 30 feet of you. Make a Wisdom (Insight) check contested by the target’s Charisma (Deception) check. If your check succeeds, you have advantage on attack rolls and ability checks against the target until the end of your next turn.

name: Historian
sheetText: When you take the Help action to aid another creature’s ability check, you can make a DC 15 Intelligence (History) check. On a success, that creature’s check gains a bonus equal to your proficiency bonus, as you share pertinent advice and historical examples. To receive this bonus, the creature must be able to understand what you’re saying.

name: Investigator
sheetText: You can take the Search action as a bonus action.

name: Medic
sheetText: During a short rest, you can clean and bind the wounds of up to six willing beasts and humanoids. Make a DC 15 Wisdom (Medicine) check for each creature. On a success, if a creature spends a Hit Die during this rest, that creature can forgo the roll and instead regain the maximum number of hit points the die can restore. A creature can do so only once per rest, regardless of how many Hit Dice it spends.

name: Menacing
sheetText: You can attempt to demoralize one humanoid you can see within 30 feet of you that can see and hear you. Make a Charisma (Intimidation) check contested by the target’s Wisdom (Insight) check. If your check succeeds, the target is frightened until the end of your next turn. If your check fails, the target can’t be frightened by you in this way for 1 hour.

name: Naturalist
sheetText: You can cast detect poison and disease without expending a spell slot.

name: Perceptive
sheetText: Being in a lightly obscured area doesn’t impose disadvantage on your Wisdom (Perception) checks if you can both see and hear.

name: Performer
sheetText: While performing, you can try to distract one humanoid you can see who can see and hear you. Make a Charisma (Performance) check contested by the humanoid’s Wisdom (Insight) check. If your check succeeds, you grab the humanoid’s attention enough that it makes Wisdom (Perception) and Intelligence (Investigation) checks with disadvantage until you stop performing.

name: Quick-Fingered
sheetText: You can make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check to plant something on someone else, conceal an object on a creature, lift a purse, or take something from a pocket.

name: Silver-Tongued
sheetText: You can attempt to deceive one humanoid you can see within 30 feet of you that can see and hear you. Make a Charisma (Deception) check contested by the target’s Wisdom (Insight) check. If your check succeeds, your movement doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks from the target and your attack rolls against it have advantage; both benefits last until the end of your next turn or until you use this ability on a different target. If your check fails, the target can’t be deceived by you in this way for 1 hour.

name: Stealthy
sheetText: If you are hidden, you can move up to 10 feet in the open without revealing yourself if you end the move in a position where you’re not clearly visible.

name: Survivalist
sheetText: You can cast alarm spell once without expending a spell slot.

name: Theologian
sheetText: You can cast detect evil and good once without expending a spell slot.

name: Barbed Hide
sheetText: You can cause small barbs to protrude all over your body or cause them to retract. At the start of each of your turns while the barbs are out, you deal 1d6 piercing damage to any creature grappling you or any creature grappled by you.

name: Critter Friend
sheetText: You learn the speak with animals spell and can cast it at will, without expending a spell slot. You also learn the animal friendship spell, and you can cast it once with this feat, without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a long rest. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

name: Dragon Wings
sheetText: With your wings, you have a flying speed of 20 feet if you aren’t wearing heavy armor and aren’t exceeding your carrying capacity.

name: Grudge-Bearer
sheetText: During the first round of any combat against your chosen foes, your attack rolls against any of them have advantage. When any of your chosen foes makes an opportunity attack against you, it makes the attack roll with disadvantage. And whenever you make an Intelligence (Arcana, History, Nature, or Religion) check to recall information about your chosen foes, you add double your proficiency bonus to the check, even if you’re not normally proficient.

name: Human Determination
sheetText: When you make an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw, you can do so with advantage. Once you use this ability, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

name: Orcish Aggression
sheetText: You can move up to your speed toward an enemy of your choice that you can see or hear. You must end this move closer to the enemy than you started.

name: Wonder Maker
sheetText: When you make a check using your proficiency with tinker’s tools, you add double your proficiency bonus to the check. When you make a device with your Tinker trait, you have the following additional options for what you make: Alarm, Calculator, Lifter, Timekeeper or Weather Sensor.

name: Telekinetic
source: Unearthed Arcana: Fighter, Rogue, and Wizard
sheetText:
You learn the mage hand cantrip and can cast it without verbal or somatic components, and you can make the spectral hand invisible.
			As a bonus action, you can try to shove one creature you can see within 5 feet of the spectral hand created by your mage hand spell. When you do so, the target must succeed on a DC{{telekinetic feat:dc}} Strength saving throw or be pushed 5 feet away from you.

name: Telepathic
source: Unearthed Arcana: Fighter, Rogue, and Wizard
sheetText: You can communicate telepathically with any creature you can see within 30 feet of you. If it understands at least one language, it can respond to you telepathically.

name: Metabolic Control
sheetText:
If your Psionic Talent die is available, you can take an action to channel your psionic power to nourish yourself for the next 24 hours, as if you consumed sufficient food and water for a day. When you take this action, your Psionic Talent die decreases by one die size.
			If your Psionic Talent die is available, you can meditate for 1 minute, at the end of which you gain the benefits of finishing a short rest, and your Psionic Talent die decreases by one die size. You can’t meditate in this way again until you finish a long rest.

name: Tower of Iron Will
sheetText: After you or another creature you can see within 30 feet of you fails a saving throw, you can roll your Psionic Talent die and add the number rolled to the saving throw, potentially causing it to succeed.

name: Wild Talent
sheetText:
Your Psionic Talent die is a d{{ua:psionic-talent-die:size}}. If you roll the highest number on your Psionic Talent die, it decreases by one die size after the roll. If it’s a d4 and you roll a 4, it becomes unusable until you finish a long rest.
			If you roll a 1 on your Psionic Talent die, it increases by one die size after the roll, up to its starting size.
			Whenever you finish a long rest, your Psionic Talent die resets to its starting size.

name: Artificer Initiate
sheetText:
You can cast this feat’s 1st-level spell without a spell slot or using any spell slots you have. This feat's cantrip and spell use Intelligence as your spellcasting ability.
			You can use this feat’s type of tool as a spellcasting focus for any spell you cast that uses Intelligence as its spellcasting ability.

name: Chef
sheetText:
As part of a short rest, you can cook special food, provided you have ingredients and cook’s utensils on hand. You can prepare enough of this food for {{ua:chef:prepare}} creatures. At the end of the short rest, any creature who eats the food and spends one or more Hit Dice to regain hp regains an extra 1d8 hp.
			With one hour of work or when you finish a long rest, you can cook {{proficiency}} treats. These special treats last 8 hours after being made. A creature can use a bonus action to eat one of those treats to gain {{proficiency}} temporary hp.

name: Crusher
sheetText:
Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack that deals bludgeoning damage, you can move it 5 feet to an unoccupied space, provided the target is no more than one size larger than you.
			When you score a critical hit that deals bludgeoning damage to a creature, attack rolls against that creature are made with advantage until the end of your next turn.

name: Fighting Initiate
sheetText: Your martial training has helped you develop a particular style of fighting.

name: Gunner
sheetText:
You ignore the loading property of firearms.
			Being within 5 feet of a hostile creature doesn’t impose disadvantage on your ranged attack rolls.

name: Metamagic Adept
sheetText: You can use only one Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it, unless the option says otherwise. You gain 2 sorcery points to spend on Metamagic. You regain all spent sorcery points when you finish a long rest.

name: Piercer
sheetText:
Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack that deals piercing damage against a creature, you can reroll one of the attack’s damage dice, and you must use the new roll.
			When you score a critical hit that deals piercing damage to a creature, you can roll one additional damage die when determining the extra piercing damage the target takes.

name: Poisoner
sheetText:
When you make a damage roll, you ignore resistance to poison damage.
			You can coat a weapon in poison as a bonus action, instead of an action.
			With one hour of work using a poisoner’s kit and expending 50 gp worth of materials, you can create {{proficiency}} doses of potent poison. Once applied, the poison retains potency for 1 minute, or until you hit with the weapon. When a weapon coated in this poison deals damage to a creature, that creature must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution save or take 2d8 damage and become poisoned until the end of your next turn.

name: Shield Training
sheetText:
In combat, you can don or doff a shield as the free object interaction on your turn.
			If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic feature, you can use a shield as a spellcasting focus.

name: Slasher
sheetText:
Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack that deals slashing damage, you can reduce the speed of the target by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.
			When you score a critical hit that deals slashing damage to a creature, you grievously wound it. Until the start of your next turn, the target has disadvantage on all attack rolls.

name: Tandem Tactician
sheetText:
You can use the Help action as a bonus action.
			When you use the Help action to aid an ally in attacking a creature, increase the range of the Help action by 10 feet. Additionally, you can help two allies targeting the same creature within range when you use the Help action this way.

name: Tracker
sheetText:
You can cast the hunter’s mark spell once without expending a spell slot or using any spell slots you have, using Wisdom as your spellcasting ability. 
			You have advantage on Survival checks to track creatures.

name: Gift of the Chromatic Dragon
sheetText:
As a bonus action, you can touch a simple or martial weapon and infuse it with one of the following damage types: acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison. For the next minute, the weapon deals an extra 1d4 damage of the chosen type when it hits. After you use this ability, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest. 
			When you take acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison damage, you can use your reaction to give yourself resistance to that instance of damage. You can use this reaction a number of times equal to {{proficiency}}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

choices.name: Spellcasting Ability (Gift of the Metallic Dragon)
choices.supports: UA20210414 Gift of the Metallic Dragon

name: Gift of the Gem Dragon
sheetText: When you take damage from a creature that is within 10 feet of you, you can emanate telekinetic energy. The creature that dealt damage to you must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC{{ua:gotgd:dc}}) or take 2d8 force damage and be pushed 10 feet away from you.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Gift of the Metallic Dragon)
choices.supports: UA20210414 Gift of the Gem Dragon

name: Knight of the Crown
sheetText: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll against another creature that is within 5 feet of you, you can grant advantage on the attack roll.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Knight of the Crown)

name: Knight of the Sword
sheetText: Immediately after you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you fail an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw, you can expend a Hit Die. The saving throw increases by an amount equal to a roll of that Hit Die, potentially turning a failure into a success. Once you turn a failed saving throw into a successful one using this feat, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest.

choices.name: Saving Throw Proficiency (Knight of the Sword)

name: Knight of the Rose
sheetText: When you roll initiative, you can choose up to three other creatures you can see within 30 ft. of you. Each creature can gain temporary hit points equal to a roll of your Hit Die + {{ua:bolstering rally:thp}}.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Knight of the Rose)

name: Adept of the Black Robes
source: Unearthed Arcana: Heroes of Krynn Revisited

name: Adept of the Red Robes
source: Unearthed Arcana: Heroes of Krynn Revisited

name: Adept of the White Robes
source: Unearthed Arcana: Heroes of Krynn Revisited

name: Divinely Favored
source: Unearthed Arcana: Heroes of Krynn Revisited

name: Initiate of High Sorcery
source: Unearthed Arcana: Heroes of Krynn Revisited

name: Knight of the Crown
source: Unearthed Arcana: Heroes of Krynn Revisited

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Knight of the Crown)

name: Knight of the Sword
source: Unearthed Arcana: Heroes of Krynn Revisited

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Knight of the Sword)

name: Knight of the Rose
source: Unearthed Arcana: Heroes of Krynn Revisited

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Knight of the Rose)

name: Squire of Solamnia
source: Unearthed Arcana: Heroes of Krynn Revisited
sheetText:
If the maneuver requires a saving throw, the save DC is {{ua:squire of solamnia:dc}}.
			Whenever you finish a long rest, you can replace the maneuvers you learned from this feat and feats that have this feat as a prerequisite with the other maneuvers offered by them.

name: Ember of the Fire Giant
sheetText: You can replace one of your attacks with a magical burst of flame. Each creature of your choice within 15 feet of you that can see you must make a DC{{ua:ember of the fire giant:dc}} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 2d6 + {{proficiency}} fire damage and is blinded until the start of your next turn, or half as much damage and no additional effects on a successful save.

name: Fury of the Frost Giant
sheetText: When a creature hits you with an attack roll, you can retaliate with a burst of magical ire. The creature must succeed on a DC{{ua:fury of the frost giant:dc}} Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of you until the start of its next turn.

name: Outsized Might
sheetText: You have advantage on saving throws against being moved or knocked prone.

name: Agent of Order
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText: Once per turn when you damage a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself, you can deal an extra 1d8 force damage to the target, and it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw DC{{ua:agent of order:dc}} or be restrained by spectral bindings until the start of your next turn.

name: Baleful Scion
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can also deal 1d6+{{proficiency}} necrotic damage to it, and you regain a number of hit points equal to this necrotic damage dealt.

name: Cartomancer
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText: You can use a deck of cards as your spellcasting focus. When you use the deck as a focus to cast a spell that deals damage, roll a d4. You gain a bonus to one damage roll of the spell equal to the number rolled. This bonus applies to one creature of your choice that you can see damaged by the spell.

name: Cohort of Chaos
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText:
You can release a chaotic flare, rolling on the Chaotic Flares table to determine what happens. A flare lasts until the end of your next turn, and a new flare can’t occur until after the first flare ends. 
			Additionally, when you roll a 1 or a 20 on an attack roll or a saving throw, roll on the table as normal to determine the effects.

name: Ember of the Fire Giant
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText: You can replace a single attack with a magical burst of flame. Each creature of your choice within 15 feet of you that can see you must make a Dexterity saving throw DC{{ua:searing ignition:dc}}. On a failed save, a creature takes 1d8 + {{proficiency}} fire damage, and it is blinded until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage and isn’t blinded.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Ember of the Fire Giant)

name: Fury of the Frost Giant
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText: Immediately after a creature you can see within 30 feet of you hits you with an attack roll and deals damage, you can retaliate with a conjured blast of ice. The creature must make a Constitution saving throw DC{{ua:frigid retaliation:dc}}. On a failed save, a creature takes 1d8 + {{proficiency}} cold damage, and its speed is halved until the end of its next turn.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Fury of the Frost Giant)

name: Guile of the Cloud Giant
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText: When a creature you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can give yourself resistance to that attack’s damage. You then teleport to an unoccupied space that you can see within 30 feet of yourself.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Guile of the Cloud Giant)

name: Keenness of the Stone Giant
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText: You can touch a rock that can fit in the palm of your hand and imbue it with magic. While the rock is imbued with magic and you are wielding it, the rock is a magic ranged weapon with which you’re proficient, and it has the thrown property with a normal range of 60 feet and a long range of 180 feet. On a hit, the rock deals 1d10 bludgeoning damage, and if the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw DC{{ua:stone throw:dc}} or be knocked prone. The magic remains in the rock until you hit with it or finish a long rest.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Fury of the Frost Giant)

name: Outlands Envoy
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse

name: Planar Wanderer
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse

name: Righteous Heritor
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText: When you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can dull its suffering and reduce the damage it takes by 1d10 + {{proficiency}}.

name: Rune Carver Apprentice
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse

name: Rune Carver Adept
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText:
Whenever you cast a rune spell or a spell of a school of magic associated with the spell you marked on an object from your Rune Carver Apprentice feat, you can invoke one of the following runic powers of your choice: 
			• Battle Runes. Choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. Until the end of that creature’s next turn, it has advantage on the next attack roll it makes. 
			• Healing Runes. Choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. That creature gains {{level}} temporary hit points. 
			• Runic Winds. Choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. Until the end of that creature’s turn, its movement doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks, and its walking speed increases by 10 feet. 
			You can't invoke runic power more than once per spell you cast.

name: Scion of Elemental Air
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText: You can gain a {{speed}}ft. flying speed until the end of your turn. If you are airborne at the end of your turn after using this movement and aren’t held aloft by other means, you fall.

name: Scion of Elemental Earth
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText: You can conjure a bulwark of earth that provides half cover to you or a creature of your choice within 30 feet of yourself. The bulwark remains until the start of your next turn.

name: Scion of Elemental Fire
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText: You can cast produce flame.

name: Scion of Elemental Water
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText: You can create a forceful surge of water directed at a creature within 15 feet of you that you can see. The target must make a Strength saving throw DC{{ua:wave surge:dc}}, and a creature can choose to fail this saving throw. On a failure, the target is pushed up to 10 feet away from you or pulled up to 10 feet toward you (your choice). The water vanishes immediately after the creature succeeds or fails.

name: Scion of the Outer Planes
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse

choices.name: Planar Infusion (Scion of the Outer Planes)
Spellcasting Ability (Scion of the Outer Planes)

name: Soul of the Storm Giant
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText: You surround yourself in an aura of magical wind and lightning that extends 10 feet from you in every direction but not through total cover. The aura lasts until the start of your next turn or until you are incapacitated. While the aura is active, attack rolls against you have disadvantage, and whenever a creature starts its turn within the aura, you can force the creature to make a Strength saving throw DC{{ua:maelstrom aura:dc}}. On a failed save, the creature’s speed is halved until the start of its next turn.

choices.name: Ability Score Increase (Soul of the Storm Giant)

name: Strike of the Giants
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse

name: Vigor of the Hill Giant
source: Unearthed Arcana: Wonders of the Multiverse
sheetText: When you are subjected to an effect that would move you at least 5 feet or knock you prone, you can steady yourself. You are then neither moved nor knocked prone.

name: Vampire Exultation
sheetText: Once per rest you can gain a flying speed of 30 ft for 10 minutes.

name: Servo Crafting
sheetText: You can cast find familiar as a ritual and can forgo one of your attacks to attack with your servo.

name: Quicksmithing
sheetText: You learn two free rituals of the first level and can learn rituals that are of level {{level:half:up}} or lower costing 50 gp and 2h per spell level (Spellcasting ability is intelligence). You gain proficiency with the quicksmith's tools and can craft quicksmith contraptions (AC 5, 1 HP) for 10 gp and 1 hour per contraption. Up to a maximum of 3 contraptions that function for up to 24 hours.
